info.title: Rewind API
info.description:
Personal data aggregation API. Syncs and serves data from Strava, Last.fm, Discogs, Plex, Letterboxd, and Trakt.

## Domains

| Domain | Source | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| **Listening** | Last.fm | Scrobbles, top artists/albums/tracks, streaks, stats |
| **Running** | Strava | Activities, splits, gear, personal records, year summaries |
| **Watching** | Plex, Letterboxd | Movies, TV shows, watch history, ratings, reviews |
| **Collecting** | Discogs, Trakt | Vinyl/CD collection, physical media, wantlist |

## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer token. There are two key types:

- **Read keys** (`rw_live_...`) — access all GET endpoints
- **Admin keys** (`rw_admin_...`) — access all endpoints including sync triggers and data management

Pass your key in the Authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer rw_live_...`

## Pagination

List endpoints return paginated responses:

```json
{
  "data": [...],
  "pagination": { "page": 1, "limit": 20, "total": 150, "total_pages": 8 }
}
```

The activity feed uses cursor-based pagination instead.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Check the API is up
curl https://api.rewind.rest/v1/health

# Fetch recent scrobbles (requires read key)
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer rw_live_..." \
  https://api.rewind.rest/v1/listening/recent

# Fetch running stats
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer rw_live_..." \
  https://api.rewind.rest/v1/running/stats
```

## Errors

All errors follow the same envelope:

```json
{ "error": "Not found", "status": 404 }
```

Common status codes: 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 404 (not found), 500 (server error).

tags.name: Listening
tags.description: Last.fm scrobbles, top charts, streaks, and stats.

tags.name: Running
tags.description: Strava activities, splits, gear, records, and year summaries.

tags.description: Image proxy with on-the-fly transforms via Cloudflare Images.

tags.description: API key management, sync triggers, and data administration. Requires admin key.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Inbound webhook receivers for Strava and Plex. No auth required.

bearerAuth.description: API key. Read keys (rw_live_...) access GET endpoints. Admin keys (rw_admin_...) access all endpoints.

get.description: Returns API health status and current timestamp.
Returns the latest sync status for each data domain, including last sync time, items synced, duration, and 24-hour error rate.
Returns the currently playing or most recently scrobbled track from Last.fm.
Returns the most recent scrobbles. Supports date filtering via date, from, and to params.
Returns top artists for a given time period.
Returns top albums for a given time period.
Returns top tracks for a given time period.
Returns listening statistics. Supports optional date filtering to scope stats to a time period.
Returns paginated scrobble history with optional filters.
Returns paginated list of all artists.
Returns paginated list of all albums.
Returns detailed information about an artist including top albums and tracks.
Returns detailed information about an album including tracks.
Returns daily scrobble counts for a given year.
Returns monthly trend data for a given metric.
Returns current and longest listening streaks.
Returns year-in-review listening data. Optionally pass ?month=N to scope to a single month.

schema.description: Optional month (1-12) to scope results to a single month

parameters.description: Optional month (1-12) to scope results to a single month
parameters.name: month

get.description: Returns genre breakdown over time, grouped by period. Designed for stacked bar charts.

schema.description: Max genres to return (rest grouped as "Other")

parameters.description: Max genres to return (rest grouped as "Other")
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns all listening filters.

post.description: Creates a new listening filter.

delete.description: Deletes a listening filter by ID.

post.description: Backfills missing images for albums and/or artists.

post.summary: Enrich tracks with Apple Music URLs
post.description: Enriches unenriched tracks with Apple Music deep links and preview audio via iTunes Search API.

get.description: Returns aggregate lifetime running statistics.
Returns running summaries for all years.
Returns running summary for a specific year.
Returns personal records for standard race distances.
Returns the last N activities (default 5, max 20). Supports date filtering via date, from, and to params.
Returns a paginated, filterable list of running activities.
Returns a single activity by Strava ID.
Returns per-mile splits for a specific activity.
Returns all gear/shoes with activity counts.
Returns daily activity heatmap data for a given year.
Returns year-over-year cumulative distance data for charting.
Returns pace over time with a rolling weighted average.
Returns run frequency by hour of day.
Returns elevation data with cumulative totals.
Returns cities where runs occurred with counts and distances.
Returns current and longest running streaks.
Returns all race activities, optionally filtered by distance category.
Returns the Eddington number and progress toward the next target.

example.explanation: You have run at least 11 miles on at least 11 days

get.description: Returns a year-in-review summary with monthly breakdown and top runs.
Returns most recently watched movies. Supports date filtering via date, from, and to params.

movie.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
movie.tagline: One man's struggle to take it easy.
movie.summary: A high school wise guy is determined to have a day off from school, despite what the Principal thinks of that.

data.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off

data.summary: A high school wise guy is determined to have a day off from school, despite what the Principal thinks of that.

example.title: Ferris Bueller's Day Off

example.summary: A high school wise guy is determined to have a day off from school, despite what the Principal thinks of that.

get.description: Returns aggregate watching statistics. Supports optional date filtering to scope stats to a time period.
Returns genre breakdown for all watched movies.
Returns decade breakdown for all watched movies.
Returns top directors by movie count.
Returns daily watch counts for a given year.
Returns weekly or monthly watch counts. Supports date filtering via from/to params.
Returns paginated list of TV shows.

data.title: Band of Brothers
data.summary: The story of Easy Company during WWII.

get.description: Returns full details for a single TV show including watched episodes.

example.title: Band of Brothers
example.summary: The story of Easy Company during WWII.

get.description: Returns watched episodes for a specific season of a show.
Returns paginated list of movies with user ratings.
Returns paginated list of movies with user reviews.

data.review: An absolute masterpiece of tension and camaraderie.

get.description: Returns aggregate stats and top-rated movies for a specific year.

post.description: Create a manual watch event by TMDB ID or title search.

put.description: Update fields on an existing watch event.

delete.description: Delete a watch event by ID.

post.description: Backfill missing images for movies and/or shows from TMDB.

get.description: Validates a Strava webhook subscription by echoing back the hub.challenge parameter. No auth required.

post.description: Receives Strava webhook events for activity creates, updates, and deletes. Must respond within 2 seconds. No auth required.
Receives Plex webhook events (multipart/form-data). No auth required -- uses its own verification via PLEX_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Body is parsed manually to avoid double-consumption.

get.description: Serve image via CDN redirect. Triggers the image pipeline on cache miss. Returns a 302 redirect to the CDN URL with metadata in response headers.

302.description: Redirect to CDN image URL. Metadata returned via X-ThumbHash, X-Dominant-Color, X-Accent-Color headers.

get.description: Browse available images from all configured sources for an entity. Shows the current source and whether it is an override.

put.description: Set an image override for an entity. Accepts either a JSON body with source_url or a multipart/form-data upload with an image file.

delete.description: Revert an image override and re-run the automatic pipeline to select the best available source.

post.description: Re-read images from R2 and regenerate thumbhash placeholders using the corrected encoder. Processes in batches.

get.description: Paginated, filterable, searchable Discogs vinyl collection with artist and image data.
Aggregate statistics for the vinyl collection. Supports optional date filtering to scope stats to items added within a time period.
Most recently added items to the vinyl collection. Supports date filtering via date, from, and to params.
Full detail for a single collection item including tracklist, country, and marketplace data.
Paginated Discogs wantlist with sorting.
Count of collection items grouped by primary format (Vinyl, CD, Cassette, etc).
Count of collection items grouped by genre.
Artists ranked by number of releases in the collection.
Cross-references vinyl collection with Last.fm listening data showing play counts and match confidence.
Returns daily addition counts for a given year (vinyl and media combined).

post.description: Process images for Discogs releases that are missing image metadata.

get.description: Paginated, filterable Trakt physical media collection (Blu-ray, DVD, etc) with movie metadata.
Aggregate statistics for the physical media collection by format, resolution, HDR, genre, and decade.
Most recently added items to the physical media collection. Supports date filtering via date, from, and to params.
Count of physical media items grouped by format type (bluray, dvd, etc).
Cross-references physical media collection with watch history showing watched/unwatched status.
Full detail for a single physical media item including movie metadata and watch history.

example.title: The Great Escape
example.summary: Allied prisoners of war plan for several hundred of their number to escape from a German camp during World War II.

post.description: Add a movie to the physical media collection. Resolves via TMDb, pushes to Trakt, stores locally.
Remove a movie from the physical media collection. Removes from both Trakt and local database.
Process poster images for movies in the physical media collection that are missing image metadata.

get.description: Returns recently saved or finished articles, ordered by most recent activity.

data.title: The Age of AI
data.author: Derek Thompson
data.description: How artificial intelligence is reshaping every industry.

data.author: Maxwell Zeff
data.description: The web was supposed to connect us. What went wrong?

parameters.description: 1 for starred only, 0 for unstarred only

example.text: We have built a world where distraction is the default and attention is the exception.

get.description: Returns aggregate reading statistics: total articles, finished count, currently reading, total highlights, word count, and average read time.
Returns daily counts of saved and finished articles for a given year.
Returns current and longest reading streaks (consecutive days with a finished article).
Returns tags with article counts, sorted by count descending.
Returns top source domains with article counts, sorted by count descending.
Returns year-in-review reading data.

post.description: Process missing article thumbnail images from OG metadata.

get.description: Returns a cross-domain activity feed with cursor-based pagination.

schema.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default 50)

parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

200.description: Activity feed items with cursor pagination

get.description: Returns an activity feed filtered to a single domain with cursor-based pagination.

schema.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default 50)

parameters.description: Number of items per page (1-100, default 50)
parameters.name: limit

200.description: Domain-filtered activity feed items with cursor pagination

get.description: Returns activity from a given calendar date across all years, grouped by year.

200.description: Activity grouped by year for the given date

get.description: Full-text search across all data domains (listening, running, watching, collecting).

schema.description: Filter results to a specific domain

parameters.description: Filter results to a specific domain
parameters.name: domain

get.description: Exports all data for a given domain as a downloadable JSON file. Includes all domain tables, activity feed entries, and sync runs.

200.description: Domain data export as downloadable JSON

post.description: Generate a new API key. The raw key is returned once and cannot be retrieved again.

get.description: List all API keys with prefix and hint only. Key hashes are never exposed.

delete.description: Soft-delete an API key by setting is_active to false.

post.description: Manually trigger a Last.fm listening sync. Supports scrobbles, top_lists, stats, full, and backfill sync types.
Manually trigger a Strava running activities sync.
Manually trigger a watching sync from Plex or Letterboxd. Use the source query parameter to select the source.
Manually trigger a Discogs collection sync.
Manually trigger a Trakt collection sync.
Manually trigger an Instapaper reading sync.

delete.description: Soft-delete a Strava activity by its ID and recompute running stats.

post.description: Recompute all running year summaries from activity data. Useful after bulk imports or corrections.
